input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS (iOS + Android):

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers/linkers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **MAUI workload** | Installed via .NET CLI | MAUI project templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Bundled with MAUI workload (or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Managed by MAUI workload or Android Studio | Android platform APIs |

---

## 2. Installation Steps (in order)

```bash
# 1. Install Xcode from App Store, then accept license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# 2. Install .NET SDK (use the installer from dot.net or Homebrew):
brew install --cask dotnet-sdk
# Verify:
dotnet --version

# 3. Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK, JDK, emulator):
dotnet workload install maui

# 4. (Optional) Install Android Studio for emulator management:
#    https://developer.android.com/studio
```

---

## 3. Discovering the Right Versions

```bash
# See available .NET SDKs and which you have:
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads and available updates:
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# Check which Android/iOS platforms the workload targets:
dotnet workload search maui

# Xcode version (must match Apple's minimum for your iOS target):
xcodebuild -version
```

The official compatibility matrix is at:  
[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

---

## 4. Verification

```bash
# Create and build a test app:
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode):
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android:
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios --project TestApp.csproj

# Run on Android Emulator (ensure emulator is running):
dotnet run -f net9.0-android --project TestApp.csproj
```

If builds succeed with no errors, you're good.

---

## 5. Key Notes

- **Xcode version matters** — each .NET MAUI release documents a minimum Xcode version. Check release notes.
- The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK & JDK under `~/Library/Android/`. You don't need to install them separately unless you want custom paths.
- For physical iOS devices, configure signing in the `.csproj` (`<CodesignKey>`, `<CodesignProvision>`) or use automatic provisioning via your Apple Developer account.
- **IDE (optional):** VS Code with the .NET MAUI extension, or JetBrains Rider. Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.

This is a knowledge/guidance question - I don't need to search a codebase or run commands. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
